bullets.text: A US soldier was killed and another injured in an Iranian attack in Iraq, following the deaths of two other US soldiers in Jordan.
Russia launched a major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities, resulting in eight deaths and dozens of injuries, while Kyiv continued its own assaults on Russian infrastructure.
A ferry carrying 133 passengers and crew sank off the coast of Guyana, with 67 people, including 15 children, rescued and dozens still missing.
The Northern Territory government's plan to reduce minimum block sizes in low-density suburbs from 800 to 450 square metres is raising concerns among residents.

A federal government safety net for migrant women experiencing domestic and family violence has been identified as a significant stressor, as it reportedly requires survivors to prove the genuineness of their relationship with abusers.
Australia's life dissatisfaction levels have doubled, with one in ten people reporting they are not satisfied with life, indicating new highs in 'wellbeing poverty' according to ABS data.
The EU is encountering diminishing support for new sanctions against Russia, as some national capitals are reluctant to endorse measures that could negatively impact their major companies.
The Australian dollar's decline against the New Zealand dollar is predicted to slow due to Australia's higher interest rates and a key technical support level, according to strategists.
Bond traders and Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh agree that the central bank's battle against inflation is likely far from over.
